title: Flaky Kenyan Ugali Pilaf
description: A comforting Kenyan dish with a flaky texture, infused with the warmth of yellow food coloring. Serve with your choice of dashi or stews.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use unbleached flour for best texture

instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine yellow food coloring and 1 cup of warm water to create a vibrant mixture.
Add the mixture to a large bowl with 2 cups of cold water and whisk until smooth.
In a separate bowl, combine 2 cups of all-purpose flour and a pinch of salt.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, stirring until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape each into a flat disk.
Place the disks on a pressure cooker's steamer basket, leaving space between each for even cooking.
Pour 2 cups of water into the pressure cooker and add the disk-shaped dough pieces.
Close the pressure cooker's lid and set the valve to sealing. Cook at high pressure for 10 minutes.
Quick-release the pressure and carefully remove the cooked ugali from the pressure cooker.
Serve the flaky Kenyan ugali hot, garnished with your choice of dashi or stews.
